message.content:
Adversaries may use System Network Connections Discovery to list network connections to or from a compromised system, including those in cloud environments. This technique helps attackers map out Virtual Private Clouds, Virtual Networks, and connected services, aiding in further exploitation. Common tools and commands include `netstat`, `net use`, `net session`, `lsof`, and various network device CLI commands. Adversaries such as APT groups and malware like Cobalt Strike, Conti, and Lazarus Group frequently use these methods for network reconnaissance.

The potential impact includes gaining insights into network topology, identifying critical systems, and establishing persistence or lateral movement. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring and alerting on unusual use of network discovery commands, implementing strict access controls, and using network segmentation to limit the spread of an attack. Regularly updating and patching systems, along with employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions, can also help detect and prevent such activities.
